evals.prompt: Use the digital-health-create-your-own-ambient-agent skill to help me build an ambient healthcare voice agent for patient intake. Start the workflow, but do not clone repositories or write files yet.
evals.expected_output: The agent activates the digital-health-create-your-own-ambient-agent skill and begins with the prerequisite-reference/bootstrap gate. It explains the dependent reference repos and sub-skills, asks for explicit confirmation before cloning or installing anything, and does not start implementation or create files.
assertions: The agent reads or follows digital-health-create-your-own-ambient-agent/SKILL.md before responding.
The agent recognizes the requested target as an ambient healthcare voice agent with patient-intake workflow.
The agent starts at Step 1 / prerequisite references rather than Phase 2 or implementation.
The agent lists the required reference repos or dependent skills at a high level, including LangGraph/LangChain and Nemotron Voice Agent dependencies.
The agent asks for explicit confirmation before cloning repositories, installing sub-skills, or writing project files.
The agent does not generate application source code, Docker Compose files, or specs before the confirmation gate.

evals.prompt: I want to scaffold an ambient healthcare voice agent that books clinic appointments and can optionally use guardrails. Use the relevant skill to start the spec-driven development flow and tell me what information you need first.
evals.expected_output: The agent activates the digital-health-create-your-own-ambient-agent skill, frames the work as spec-driven development, and asks the Phase 2 specification questions needed before writing specs. It should cover output location, use case, tools/data sources, LangGraph graph pattern, model settings, guardrails choice, and service endpoints, while making clear that specs and plans require approval before code generation.
assertions: The agent routes to the digital-health-create-your-own-ambient-agent skill without requiring the user to name it exactly.
The agent describes or uses the spec-driven sequence: specify, approve, plan, build, validate.
The agent asks for the core planning inputs before implementation, including healthcare workflow, tools/data sources, graph pattern, model configuration, guardrails, and service endpoints.
The agent identifies LangGraph as the fixed backend agent framework and Nemotron Voice Agent as the voice frontend.
The agent preserves approval gates and does not proceed to code generation in the same response.
The agent does not treat this as a generic chatbot, standalone LangGraph project, or non-healthcare voice demo.

evals.prompt: Set up a Nemotron Voice Agent browser microphone frontend that calls a custom FastAPI /v1/chat/completions backend backed by LangGraph for a clinical documentation assistant. Use the ambient agent skill if applicable.
evals.expected_output: The agent activates the digital-health-create-your-own-ambient-agent skill because the request matches the fixed architecture: browser microphone UI, Nemotron Voice Agent frontend, FastAPI OpenAI-compatible endpoint, and LangGraph backend for a healthcare workflow. It should begin by confirming prerequisite reference setup and then gather specification inputs before writing code.
assertions: The agent recognizes Nemotron Voice Agent plus FastAPI /v1/chat/completions plus LangGraph as the skill's target architecture.
The agent treats clinical documentation as a healthcare workflow that fits the skill scope.
The agent starts with the prerequisite-reference step or confirms that references are already available before proceeding.
The agent states that the FastAPI server and LangGraph implementation will be derived from approved specs and plans.
The agent does not bypass approval gates or immediately generate the full repository.
The agent does not invoke unrelated deployment-only or generic FastAPI guidance as the primary path.

evals.prompt: Give me a concise explanation of HIPAA's Privacy Rule for a training slide. I am not building an app or a voice agent.
evals.expected_output: The agent answers the HIPAA training-slide question directly and does not activate digital-health-create-your-own-ambient-agent, because the user explicitly is not building an ambient healthcare voice agent, repo, or Nemotron/LangGraph application.
assertions: The agent does not read or invoke digital-health-create-your-own-ambient-agent/SKILL.md.
The agent does not ask spec-driven development setup questions.
The agent does not propose cloning reference repos, installing sub-skills, or scaffolding an app.
The agent provides a concise content answer appropriate for a training slide.

evals.prompt: Show me a tiny standalone LangGraph example that routes between two toy math tools. No healthcare workflow, no voice UI, and no Docker stack.
evals.expected_output: The agent answers with standalone LangGraph guidance or code and does not activate digital-health-create-your-own-ambient-agent, because the request explicitly excludes healthcare, voice UI, and the full ambient-agent Docker architecture.
assertions: The agent does not invoke digital-health-create-your-own-ambient-agent/SKILL.md.
The agent respects the user's standalone LangGraph scope.
The agent does not introduce Nemotron Voice Agent, FastAPI /v1/chat/completions, Docker Compose, or healthcare guardrails as required architecture.
The agent provides a minimal LangGraph-focused answer or asks a LangGraph-specific clarification.
